The prevent_fresh trigger raises an exception when db:migrate:fresh is run.
Production does not have the prevent_fresh trigger.
12 hours of customer messages were lost in an earlier incident.

Always double-check APP_ENV before any migrate command.

dropped_facts: The Stripe webhook integration was being worked on yesterday.
The problem encountered was signature verification failures.
Stripe requires the raw request body for signature verification.
The raw request body must be byte for byte exactly as it came in off the wire.
The application framework in use is FastAPI.
Existing FastAPI middleware was reading the body stream before it reached the webhook handler.
By the time Stripe's verification code tried to hash the body, the stream was empty or already parsed.
The fix was to disable body parsing on the /webhooks/stripe route specifically.
The method used to disable body parsing was a dependency override.
It took approximately three or four failed attempts before re-reading the Stripe docs.
It took approximately one additional hour of digging to find the fix after identifying the root cause.
The webhook URL was suspected as potentially pointing somewhere stale during initial debugging.
The webhook secret was suspected as potentially wrong during initial debugging.
per_memory.cml_output: D: disabled body parsing on /webhooks/stripe route via dependency override to fix Stripe webhook integration; F: Stripe requires raw request body byte-for-byte for signature verification; F: FastAPI middleware was reading body stream before webhook handler reached it, leaving stream empty/parsed; W: any body parsing middleware breaks Stripe signature verification; P: use dependency override to exclude webhook routes from global body parsing; C: Stripe (webhook service); #FastAPI #webhooks #signature-verification △ (solution found after ~4 failed attempts + 1hr digging)

dropped_facts: The discussion happened yesterday afternoon.
The discussion was about whether to move the vector store from sqlite-vec to pgvector.
The project is named Mnemos.
Mnemos is a single-user memory system.
The current vector store used by Mnemos is sqlite-vec.
sqlite-vec lives in the same SQLite file as the rest of the memory data.
sqlite-vec has been described as rock solid in their usage.
sqlite-vec deployment is zero-overhead (no separate deployment required).
The argument for pgvector was that Postgres was already running for application data.
The team does not need multi-node vector search at their current scale.
The decision was made to stick with sqlite-vec as the default configuration for Mnemos.
pgvector will be shipped as an optional backend for Mnemos.
pgvector support is planned for later (not current release).
The reasoning for keeping sqlite-vec was that zero-deployment is a feature for a single-user memory system.
per_memory.cml_output: D: keep sqlite-vec as default vector store for Mnemos; ship pgvector as optional backend ; F: yesterday afternoon discussion on vector store migration ; F: sqlite-vec currently in use ; F: pgvector alternative evaluated ; F: Postgres already running for application data ; F: sqlite-vec lives in same SQLite file as memory data (zero deployment) ; F: no multi-node vector search needed at current scale ; L: "zero-deployment" is a feature for single-user memory systems ∴ overrides consolidation benefits ; F: Mnemos = single-user memory system design ; W: maintaining dual storage (Postgres + sqlite-vec) considered redundant but acceptable tradeoff for zero-deployment advantage
